---
title: "The Story of Scurvy"
format:
  html: default
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

Scurvy was a serious disease that affected sailors during long sea voyages because they lacked fresh fruits and vegetables. Over time, scientists developed different explanations for what caused the disease and how to prevent it.

In 1747, James Lind conducted an experiment and found that sailors who ate lemons recovered from scurvy. At the time, people only knew that lemons worked, but they did not understand the reason. The first DAG represents this simple idea: lemons prevent scurvy.

Later, scientists developed a wrong explanation. They believed that the acid in lemons killed bacteria in food, and that this prevented scurvy. Because of this belief, lemons were sometimes replaced with other acidic substances like vinegar or limes. However, these did not work well, and scurvy continued to appear. The second DAG shows this incorrect chain: acid → bacteria in food → scurvy.

Finally, in the early 20th century, researchers discovered the real cause. Vitamin C in citrus fruits helps the body produce collagen, which is important for healthy tissues. When people do not get enough Vitamin C, collagen production fails and scurvy develops. The final DAG shows the correct mechanism: Vitamin C → collagen synthesis → scurvy.

This story shows how scientific understanding can change over time as new evidence helps us better understand the true cause of a problem.

```{python}
import daft
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.dpi": 160,
    "savefig.dpi": 300,
    "font.size": 12,
    "font.family": "DejaVu Sans",
})
```

## DAG 1: 1747 Understanding

```{python}
COLORS = {
    "treatment_fill": "#FFF1C9",
    "outcome_fill":   "#E8EEF6",
    "stroke":         "#1F2D3D",
    "edge":           "#1F2D3D",
    "title":          "#111827",
}

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=160, alternate_style="outer")

pgm.add_node(
    "lemons", "Lemons", 1.0, 1.0,
    aspect=2.40, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["treatment_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_node(
    "scurvy", "Scurvy", 3.0, 1.0,
    aspect=2.40, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["outcome_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_edge("lemons", "scurvy")

pgm.render()
pgm.figure.set_size_inches(7.0, 2.4)
pgm.ax.set_title("Simplified Lind’s Model (1747)", fontsize=15, pad=14, color=COLORS["title"])
pgm.ax.axis("off")
plt.show()
```

## DAG 2: Misguided Belief

```{python}
COLORS = {
    "cause_fill":     "#FDE2E2",
    "mediator_fill":  "#D9F2FF",
    "outcome_fill":   "#E8EEF6",
    "edge":           "#1F2D3D",
    "title":          "#111827",
}

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=160, alternate_style="outer")

pgm.add_node(
    "acid", "Acid", 1.0, 3.0,
    aspect=2.10, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["cause_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_node(
    "bacteria", "Bacteria in Food", 2.35, 2.0,
    aspect=3.30, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["mediator_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_node(
    "scurvy", "Scurvy", 3.60, 1.0,
    aspect=2.40, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["outcome_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_edge("acid", "bacteria")
pgm.add_edge("bacteria", "scurvy")

pgm.render()
pgm.figure.set_size_inches(7.6, 4.6)
pgm.ax.set_title("Updated Bacterial Theory (Late 19th Century)", fontsize=15, pad=14, color=COLORS["title"])
pgm.ax.axis("off")
plt.show()
```

## DAG 3: Modern Understanding

```{python}
COLORS = {
    "cause_fill":     "#DFF7E3",
    "process_fill":   "#FFF1C9",
    "outcome_fill":   "#E8EEF6",
    "edge":           "#1F2D3D",
    "title":          "#111827",
}

pgm = daft.PGM(dpi=160, alternate_style="outer")

pgm.add_node(
    "vitc", "Vitamin C", 1.0, 3.0,
    aspect=2.55, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["cause_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_node(
    "collagen", "Collagen Synthesis", 2.55, 2.0,
    aspect=3.70, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["process_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_node(
    "scurvy", "Scurvy", 3.85, 1.0,
    aspect=2.40, scale=1.25, fontsize=13,
    plot_params={
        "facecolor": COLORS["outcome_fill"],
        "edgecolor": COLORS["edge"],
        "linewidth": 2.4,
        "alpha": 0.98
    }
)

pgm.add_edge("vitc", "collagen")
pgm.add_edge("collagen", "scurvy")

pgm.render()
pgm.figure.set_size_inches(7.8, 4.6)
pgm.ax.set_title("Simplified Modern Understanding (1930s onward)", fontsize=15, pad=14, color=COLORS["title"])
pgm.ax.axis("off")
plt.show()
```
